summary:
Generalized skin peeling started around one month into treatment for epilepsy; this pattern strongly indicates possible Toxic Epidermal Necrolysis due to medication side effects. Toxoplasmosis or other conditions were less considered as they do not match typical presentation patterns seen clinically here.
To refine Agent1’s summary while adhering strictly within two concise sentences:

Generalized skin peeling began approximately one month after starting anticonvulsants, pointing towards toxic epidermal necrolysis. Neurocysticercosis-related complications are unlikely based on timing and symptoms presented. 

This version maintains focus on critical aspects—clinical symptomatology, temporal relationship between event and intervention—and rules out alternatives effectively without extraneous details.
